fault_hypothesis: On line 61, the condition `adjusted_now > ttl` uses strict greater-than, so when `adjusted_now == ttl` (i.e., `now == ttl` and `_CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE == 0`) the entry is not expired, but the spec requires expiry when `now >= ttl`.

spec.expr: not (adjusted_now__1 == ttl__1 and __cbfl_result is not None)

constraints.intent: When adjusted_now equals ttl the function must return None (entry is expired), not the payload.
If adjusted_now equals ttl the subsequent strict-greater-than check will incorrectly allow the entry through; flag this equality as suspicious.
Verify that the comparison operator used against ttl covers the equality case (>= rather than >).
After the expiry branch is not taken, adjusted_now must be strictly less than ttl; equality should have triggered expiry.
